structural_claim: Complex systems spontaneously break symmetry to form ordered collective structures through competing interactions at multiple scales

supporting_results.structural_conclusion: Nucleons spontaneously organize into discrete quantum shells despite strong interactions, breaking spherical symmetry
Nuclei exhibit collective oscillation modes where constituents move coherently
Nuclear matter forms complex non-spherical structures through frustrated interactions at competing length scales
Rotating flows spontaneously break symmetry through hierarchical instabilities forming ordered structures
Thermal convection self-organizes into cellular patterns breaking translational symmetry
Wake flows spontaneously organize into periodic vortex patterns with specific spacing ratios
